title: Listening Skill-Drill — Form Completion (S1) — Leisure-centre swimming membership

topic_tags.S1: Phone call to register for a swimming membership at a leisure centre (surname spelling with hyphen, date of birth, address, postcode, phone, membership tier, monthly price, start date, included class, item to bring)

sections.context: A man phones Maple Vale Leisure Centre to register for a swimming membership; a membership consultant fills in a registration form (surname spelling, date of birth, address, postcode, mobile number, membership tier, monthly price, start date, an included class and an item to bring on the first visit)
sections.audio_intro: You will hear a phone call between a man who wants to join a leisure centre and a membership consultant. First, you have some time to look at questions 1 to 10. [pause:30s] Now listen carefully and answer questions 1 to 10.

audio_script.text: [sfx:phone-ring] [sfx:phone-pickup] [emotion:polite] Good morning, Maple Vale Leisure Centre, Renata speaking. How can I help you?
Oh, hello. Um, I'd like to sign up for a swimming membership, please — I've just moved to the area and I want to start using the pool.
[emotion:friendly] Lovely, I can set that up over the phone now. Let me, er, open a new form. Could I take your full name, please?

Yes, it's Tomasz Halewood-Birch. The first name is Tomasz, and the surname is the tricky bit — it's double-barrelled. It's Halewood-Birch, so that's H-A-L-E-W-O-O-D, then a hyphen, then B-I-R-C-H (Q1). There's a double-O in the middle, Halewood, not Halwood.
Great, Halewood-Birch with a hyphen, got it. And, um, what's your date of birth?
People sometimes put the twentieth, but it's the twelfth of March (Q2). The twelfth, one-two.
No problem at all — the twelfth of March. Now, your home address?
I'm at number forty Bewley Road (Q3). I think there's a Bewley Close nearby too, but mine is definitely the Road — number forty, Bewley Road.
Forty Bewley Road. And the postcode?
The postcode is M16 7QT (Q4). That's M for Manchester, one-six, then seven Q-T.
M16 7QT, thank you. Could I get a, er, contact number — a mobile is best?
Sure, my mobile is [digits:07700 924168] (Q5).

[emotion:friendly] Perfect. Now let's pick your membership. We have two main swimming options. There's the Anytime tier, which lets you swim whenever the pool is open, and there's Off-Peak Plus, which covers mornings and early afternoons on weekdays. Which would suit you?
Well… I work from home and I usually swim mid-morning, so I don't really need the evenings. I'll take the Off-Peak Plus one, not the Anytime (Q6).

Good choice. So, the Off-Peak Plus is normally thirty-eight pounds a month, but because you're joining online-ready today, there's a standing-order discount, so it'll be thirty-two pounds a month (Q7). Thirty-two, then.
Thirty-two pounds a month, that's fine. Um, when can the membership actually start?
Since the discount runs from next month, it's not the twenty-fifth of April — the first of May (Q8) is better. So the start date will be the first of May.
The first of May suits me. Oh, does the membership include any classes? My, er, physio suggested some gentle water exercise.

It does — Off-Peak Plus includes one class a week. Most people on your tier go to the aqua aerobics (Q9) session; it's, um, low-impact and great for that. There's also a lane-swimming squad, but that's for the Anytime members.
Aqua aerobics sounds ideal. Is there anything I need to bring on my first visit?
Just one thing for the pool — you'll need a swimming cap (Q10). Goggles are optional and we sell those at reception, but the cap is compulsory in our pool. So, a swimming cap on day one.
Brilliant, I'll bring one. Thanks so much for your help.
[emotion:friendly] You're very welcome. We'll see you on the first of May. Bye for now.

question_blocks.rubric:
Complete the form below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
question_blocks.title: MAPLE VALE LEISURE CENTRE — SWIMMING MEMBERSHIP REGISTRATION

answers.notes: Name spelling dictated letter-by-letter 'H-A-L-E-W-O-O-D' [hyphen] 'B-I-R-C-H'. Double-O in 'Halewood' is the trap (candidates may write 'Halwood'); the answer must keep the HYPHEN and both halves. Source: "Halewood-Birch, so that's H-A-L-E-W-O-O-D, then a hyphen, then B-I-R-C-H".

Forward distractor-drop + teen/ty-style date confusion: the wrong figure is flagged as a common mistake in a clean separate clause, then the key is stated cleanly — 'People sometimes put the twentieth, but it's the twelfth of March.' The form already prints 'March', so only the day is keyed. The twentieth is the mentioned-rejected figure; the key 'twelfth' (re-confirmed 'one-two') carries no retraction cue. Source: "it's the twelfth of March. The twelfth, one-two."

Place confusion: 'Bewley Close' is named as a nearby distractor; the customer confirms his is 'the Road'. The house number 40 is pre-printed on the form, so only the street name is keyed. Source: "number forty Bewley Road… there's a Bewley Close nearby too, but mine is definitely the Road".
Alphanumeric postcode dictated and confirmed; read as 'M, one-six, seven, Q-T'. Number/letter accuracy item. Source: "The postcode is M16 7QT. That's M for Manchester, one-six, then seven Q-T."
Mobile number dictated as a single run (wrapped [digits:] for TTS). Number accuracy item; no competing figure. Source: "my mobile is 07700 924168".

question_blocks.rubric:
Complete the form below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
question_blocks.title: MEMBERSHIP DETAILS

answers.notes: Distractor-drop + polarity: two tiers named — 'Anytime' (rejected) and 'Off-Peak Plus' (chosen, re-confirmed 'not the Anytime'). The customer swims mid-morning so does not need evenings. Hyphen counts as one word, so 'Off-Peak Plus' = two words. Source: "I'll take the Off-Peak Plus one, not the Anytime".

Number-correction: the headline price 'thirty-eight pounds' is the un-discounted figure; with the standing-order discount it becomes 'thirty-two pounds', re-confirmed 'Thirty-two, then'. The '£' is pre-printed on the form. Source: "normally thirty-eight pounds a month… so it'll be thirty-two pounds a month. Thirty-two, then."

Forward distractor-drop / date: the wrong date is named in a clean rejected clause, then the key is stated cleanly — 'it's not the twenty-fifth of April — the first of May is better', re-confirmed 'the start date will be the first of May'. The form prints 'of May', so only the day is keyed. 25th April is the mentioned-rejected date; the key 'first' carries no retraction cue. Source: "the first of May is better. So the start date will be the first of May."

Paraphrase + distractor-drop: 'gentle water exercise' (customer) is reframed as the 'aqua aerobics' session (consultant). The 'lane-swimming squad' is a distractor reserved for Anytime members. Two words. Source: "Most people on your tier go to the aqua aerobics session… There's also a lane-swimming squad, but that's for the Anytime members."

Distractor-drop: goggles are mentioned as optional (sold at reception) but the cap is 'compulsory', re-confirmed 'a swimming cap on day one'. Two words. Source: "you'll need a swimming cap. Goggles are optional… but the cap is compulsory in our pool."
